events.title: Tokugawa shogunate established
events.description: Tokugawa Ieyasu becomes shogun and establishes a centralized feudal regime in Japan, beginning a long period of internal peace and isolation (Sakoku).

events.title: Jamestown founded
events.description: English colonists establish Jamestown in Virginia, the first permanent English settlement in North America, initiating English colonial expansion.

events.title: Dutch East India Company chartered
events.description: The VOC (Dutch East India Company) is formed and becomes a dominant commercial and colonial force in Asia, establishing trading posts and naval power.

events.title: Thirty Years' War
events.description: A complex series of conflicts across Central Europe with religious and dynastic dimensions, devastating populations and reshaping the political map of Europe.

events.title: Mayflower and Plymouth Colony
events.description: Pilgrims sail to New England and establish Plymouth Colony, part of early English settlement in North America.

events.title: Mughal consolidation under Shah Jahan begins
events.description: Shah Jahan's reign marks Mughal architectural and cultural achievement, including the later construction of the Taj Mahal.

events.title: Qing consolidation in China (Manchu rule)
events.description: Manchu forces establish the Qing dynasty after seizing Beijing; the dynasty consolidates control over China and expands its frontiers.

events.title: English Civil War begins
events.description: Conflict between Royalists and Parliamentarians over governance, religion, and the power of the monarchy; leads to temporary abolition of the monarchy and the Commonwealth under Cromwell.

events.title: Taj Mahal completed (approx)
events.description: Shah Jahan completes the Taj Mahal at Agra as a monumental mausoleum for Mumtaz Mahal, exemplifying Mughal architecture and artistic synthesis.

events.title: English East India Company expands in India
events.description: The English East India Company increases trade and establishes factories and political footholds in India, setting the stage for later colonial dominance.

events.title: Maratha power grows in western India
events.description: Shivaji Maharaj and his successors establish Maratha polities that challenge Mughal authority and expand across the subcontinent.

events.title: Ottoman siege of Vienna repelled
events.description: A combined European force defeats the Ottoman siege of Vienna, marking a turning point that halts Ottoman advance into Central Europe.

events.title: Glorious Revolution in England
events.description: William of Orange invades England, James II flees, and parliamentary supremacy is reinforced, leading to constitutional changes and the Bill of Rights.

events.title: Kangxi Emperor consolidates Qing rule
events.description: Kangxi's long reign stabilizes Qing rule, expands territory, and fosters cultural and intellectual life in China.

events.title: Jahangir's reign and court culture
events.description: Jahangir consolidates Mughal administration, patronizes arts and painting, and negotiates regional alliances across South Asia.

events.title: Shah Jahan becomes Mughal emperor
events.description: Shah Jahan expands Mughal architecture and administration, commissions major building projects including the Taj Mahal.

events.title: Qing consolidation and southern campaigns
events.description: Qing forces complete conquest of Ming loyalist regimes and extend control into southern China and Taiwan, consolidating Manchu rule.

events.title: Tokugawa isolation policies (Sakoku)
events.description: Tokugawa shogunate restricts foreign contact and trade to control influence and maintain internal stability, shaping Japan's social order.

events.title: Rise of regional South Indian polities (Mysore, Maratha expansion)
events.description: Regional states such as Mysore and the Marathas expand influence as Mughal central control fluctuates, reshaping Deccan politics.

events.title: Sino-European maritime trade grows (Macao, Canton)
events.description: European trading posts and Asian merchants expand commerce in silk, tea, and silver, linking China to global trade networks.

events.title: Ayutthaya kingdom peak and regional diplomacy
events.description: Ayutthaya (Siam) consolidates regional trade networks, diplomatic ties with European merchants, and cultural exchange across Southeast Asia.

events.title: Formation of Akan states and Ashanti precursors
events.description: Akan polities in present-day Ghana consolidate trade, gold production, and political institutions that later form the Ashanti state.

events.title: Aurangzeb's reign and policy shifts
events.description: Aurangzeb expands Mughal frontiers to their greatest extent but pursues policies that intensify regional resistance and fiscal strain.

events.title: Russian eastward expansion into Siberia
events.description: Russian explorers and Cossack expeditions extend control across Siberia to the Pacific, integrating fur trade networks and indigenous contacts.

events.title: Shahaji Maharaj Bhonsle campaigns in the Deccan
events.description: Shahaji Maharaj serves various Deccan sultanates, secures jagirs around Pune and plants the Bhonsle power base that enables Shivaji's rise.

events.title: Birth of Shivaji Maharaj
events.description: Birth of Shivaji Maharaj, future founder of the Maratha polity and innovator of mountain guerrilla warfare.

events.title: Battle of Pratapgarh
events.description: Shivaji Maharaj defeats Afzal Khan of Bijapur at Pratapgarh using ambush tactics, a turning point that boosts Maratha prestige.

events.title: Pavan Khind rearguard action
events.description: Baji Prabhu Deshpande and a small Maratha force hold the pass at Pavan Khind to enable Shivaji's escape, celebrated for sacrifice and valor.

events.title: Raid on Surat
events.description: Shivaji Maharaj raids the wealthy port city of Surat, seizing treasure and undermining Mughal economic reach on the west coast.

events.title: Siege of Purandar and treaty
events.description: Mughal general Jai Singh besieges Purandar; Shivaji Maharaj negotiates the Treaty of Purandar, ceding forts but preserving his core polity.

events.title: Coronation of Shivaji Maharaj
events.description: Shivaji Maharaj is crowned Chhatrapati at Raigad, formalizing Maratha sovereignty and establishing royal institutions.

events.title: Death of Shivaji Maharaj
events.description: Shivaji Maharaj dies at Raigad; succession and continued resistance shape the next phase of Maratha–Mughal conflict.

events.title: Reign of Sambhaji Maharaj
events.description: Sambhaji Maharaj succeeds Shivaji and continues aggressive resistance against Mughal expansion, conducting raids and defensive campaigns.

events.title: Capture and execution of Sambhaji
events.description: Sambhaji Maharaj is captured by Mughal forces and executed; his death intensifies Maratha resistance under Rajaram and Tarabai.

events.title: Rajaram's resistance from Jinji
events.description: Rajaram Maharaj and Maratha commanders relocate to Jinji (Gingee) in Tamil Nadu, sustaining guerrilla resistance and tying down Mughal resources.

events.title: Shahu released and Maratha succession
events.description: After Aurangzeb's death Shahu is released from Mughal captivity; his return leads to a power struggle and eventual Peshwa ascendancy.

events.title: Baji Rao I's northern campaigns
events.description: Peshwa Baji Rao I leads rapid cavalry campaigns into Malwa, Bundelkhand and central India, expanding Maratha influence across the north.

events.title: Maratha confederacy expansion and consolidation
events.description: Maratha chiefs and Peshwas consolidate control over large swathes of India, establishing tributary relations and regional administrations.

events.title: Third Battle of Panipat
events.description: Maratha army under Sadashivrao Bhau is defeated by Ahmad Shah Durrani at Panipat, a catastrophic loss that temporarily halts Maratha northward dominance.
